comment.body: Hi Alma or anybody. So when ALMA  mentions "6071, in order to break below this, vol should cool down". It sounds be counterintuitive since vol should increase if spot drops further. So I would imagine she means that price or spot should consolidate and accept levels (therefore vol eases), before continuation of drop. Would this assessment be ok? A simple YES OR NO would suffice.

comment.body:
This might be a dumb question. But if someone could answer.

Looking at 3/21. You can get a SPY call 3.7% OTM for the same cost as a put 9.3% OTM. Why would anyone hold that put and take that risk when you can just raise cash instead risk free and afford small exposure to the upside for cheap? I’d personally feel like a complete idiot holding those puts which is why I had to ask

comment.body: I think the answer is put skew. Markets tend to grind up rather than crash up, and most people hold those puts for hedging rather than speculation. Those puts act as insurance in case of a sharp drop.
comment.raw_body: I think the answer is put skew. Markets tend to grind up rather than crash up, and most people hold those puts for hedging rather than speculation. Those puts act as insurance in case of a sharp drop.

comment.body:
Right. But in the event that crash does happen and the puts go ITM, $VIX would likely be above 25. Probably 30. You can get a $VIX $20 call for 3/18 at ~ the same cost as the put. Slightly more. Or you can the conservative route with a 3 month CD

It just doesn’t make sense given there’s at least 2 alternatives for protection that are better

comment.body:
Yeah but it’s the distance OTM. You’re choosing to pay more for less profit. The probability is high that the $VIX calls would profit more

And TLT can historically do well in a $VIX spike. July 1st to August 2nd. Market fell, TLT spiked ~ 10%. You can get TLT calls for 3/21 ~ 5% OTM for $0.17 right now. You’re paying 1/4 of the cost compared to the puts 

Puts just seem like the worst option for protection you can get right now
